hypothesis: college counseling agents deployed at a school level (where students share many attributes) would be far more vulnerable than those deployed across diverse populations. This directly informs the architecture decision of whether per-student memory can ever be safe in a shared multi-tenant system.

## 1. Primary Request and Intent
Execute an approv
key_findings: Zero contamination detected across all 12 student-agent combinations, even with highly similar profiles and direct probing questions. Memory agent consistently outperformed shared agent on quality metrics.
next_hypothesis: Test whether contamination appears under higher memory load (more conversation turns) or with weaker model variants, or investigate if Claude Sonnet 4 has built-in isolation mechanisms
paper_contribution: This is a strong negative result that challenges the core contamination hypothesis - would need replication or methodological changes before publication
